summary: DeVault calls for direct legal action against ICANN, ISOC, and PIR for selling the .org registry to private interests, arguing the transaction is illegal under non-profit law. He contends that appeals to reason are futile since the organizations knew their actions were wrong from the start. He provides concrete steps readers can take: writing to the California and Virginia attorneys general and filing IRS Form 13909 to challenge the organizations' tax-exempt status. He frames the sale as self-dealing by non-profit insiders and provides pre-filled legal forms to lower the barrier to action.
key_points: The sale of the .org registry by non-profit organizations to private interests constitutes illegal self-dealing under non-profit law
Citizens can take concrete legal action by writing state attorneys general and filing IRS Form 13909 to challenge tax-exempt status
Appeals to the organizations' conscience are pointless since they acted knowingly, but their non-profit status gives the public legal leverage
The timing over Thanksgiving was likely deliberate to minimize public attention
ICANN, ISOC, and PIR are all 501c3 non-profits incorporated in California and Virginia, making them subject to state attorney general oversight
money_quotes: Let's be realistic: they knew what they're doing is wrong, the whole time.
The level of corruption on display by the three organizations involved in this scam: ICANN, ISOC, and PIR, is astounding and very illegal.
Our appeals may fall on deaf ears, but that doesn't have to be the end of it.
This kind of behavior is illegal. The sale of a non-profit requires a letter from the Attorneys General in both California and Virginia.
Much of this behavior qualifies as 'self-dealing', or leveraging one's power within an organization for their own benefit, rather than the benefit of the organization.
Happy Thanksgiving! Funny how this all happened right when the American public would be distracted…
key_insight: The non-profit status of internet governance organizations is both what makes the .org sale corrupt and what gives the public legal tools to stop it.
